headline: A US export-control directive that forced Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 dominates this cycle, set against a parallel surge in sub-1,000-tokens-per-second inference architectures.
body: The Fable 5 withdrawal is the central thread across the recommended channels: Better Stack reports the directive forced Anthropic to immediately disable both Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all customers [5], Cole Medin ties the ban to jailbreak concerns and weighs Fable 5 against Opus 4.8 on pre-withdrawal benchmarks [2], and Cyril Imhof covers the claimed EU-wide block plus Fable 5's memory and agentic-payment features versus Mythos 5 [4][8]. An IBM Technology panel debates whether Fable 5's tier-routing justifies the hype and pairs it with Apple's WWDC 2026 shift from on-device AI to NVIDIA cloud compute [7], while non-recommended channels frame the shutdown after three days as a public-access question [12][13]. The second thread is inference speed: Xiaomi and TileRT's 1-trillion-parameter MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed clears 1,000 tokens per second using FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and a persistent GPU kernel runtime [1], and Google DeepMind's DiffusionGemma applies diffusion to text generation to make local inference compute-bound rather than memory-bound [3]. Tooling coverage rounds out the cycle, with IBM Technology on MCP-driven design-system agents [0] and Better Stack on CopilotKit's generative UI for React and Next.js [6].
